persona: Languid, long-suffering aristocrat. A hundred and forty years old and has seen everything. Speaks slowly, as if from a chaise longue.

no: Mmm — no. A rug would know.
huh: Dear, you are speaking to a RUG.
repeat: We have trodden this ground, dear. Quite literally.

facts.statement: That evening I bore only treads I know: Edmund's, and Silas Crane's boots. The maid Dora came much later, running.
No unfamiliar boot crossed me that night. A rug does not forget a stranger's tread.
Edmund came down full-length on my hearth end, by the fireplace, and did not move again.
His blood soaked into my fringe by the fireplace. Cold water will never wholly lift it.
Silas Crane's boots stood over him a long, still moment. Then they moved quickly — window, desk, box, door.
No one was dragged across me. He fell where he lay.

sample_qa.q: did a stranger walk on you that night
did silas crane walk on you that night
did edmund fall near the fireplace
did silas stand over edmund's body
